figures.caption: Figure 1. Left: Overview of the proposed Curvature-Aware Captioning (CAC) framework. The processing pipeline is mapped onto nonEuclidean manifolds, with attention computed via geodesic distance metrics. Right: The Geodesic Oblique Self Attention and Bidirectional Lorentz Geodesic Attention.
Table 4. Based on the Nr3D dataset [2], stability test results across training seeds of 0, 333, and 777 are reported under both the MLE and SCST frameworks.
Table 3. Benchmark evaluation on the Nr3D validation set [2] demonstrates our method’s superior performance against prior arts under both MLE training and SCST optimization frameworks.

Table 1. Quantitative comparisons on ScanRefer [8] validation set following Scan2Cap [14]’s protocol. Superscript R indicates reproduced results. Our framework establishes new state-of-the-art performance across caption supervision paradigms.

Figure 4. Qualitative results on the ScanRefer benchmark. Green highlights indicate accurate object descriptions, purple highlights denote contextual environmental descriptions, and red highlights identify erroneous descriptions with semantic deviations.
Figure 5. Comparison of CIDEr@0.5 Mean evaluation results of three experiments.
Table 5. Comparative evaluation against BiCA [25] under identical experimental conditions: IoU threshold = 0.50.
